# Every Tier-2 command falls back to the session-scoped identity (written by
# `identity register`, keyed on BREEZE_SESSION_ID/CLAUDE_CODE_SESSION_ID/
# CODEX_THREAD_ID) when --as is omitted, same as the Tier-1 lock/whoami commands
# already did — a real inconsistency found via user feedback: half of Tier-2
# (stage/deploy) already inferred it, half (identity/role/pipeline) required --as
# spelled out on every call even within the same session. --token is still always
# required explicitly regardless — this only ever infers WHO is asking, never
# authorizes anything by itself.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state
env BREEZE_SESSION_ID=test-session-1

# Registering writes the session-scoped identity file for "admin".
exec breeze identity register admin
cp stdout admin.token

# Register carol under a DIFFERENT simulated session — registering an identity
# always claims the session for whichever name was just registered, so doing this
# under test-session-1 would reclaim it as "carol" instead of leaving it as "admin".
env BREEZE_SESSION_ID=other-session
exec breeze identity register carol
env BREEZE_SESSION_ID=test-session-1

# role assign, with NO --as at all — must infer "admin" from the session file.
exec breeze role assign builder carol --token-file admin.token
exec breeze role list --json
stdout '"name":\s*"carol"[\s\S]*"builder"'

# identity notify similarly infers the session identity.
exec breeze identity notify off
exec breeze role list --json
stdout '"name":\s*"admin"[\s\S]*"notifyOptOut":\s*true'

exec breeze stop
